rationale: Built the binary and empirically verified all three claims. (1) `mindspec panel create <slug> --spec <id> --target <ref> [--bead <id>] [--round N]` matches the real cobra Use string and actual runtime behavior byte-for-byte (verified round-1 create and round-2/3 re-panel with co-bumped round+reviewed_head_sha, prior stub edits preserved). (2) The skill's description of the BRIEF stub 'create' writes — Summary / Files in Scope / Prior-Round Asks / Lens headings — matches internal/panel/create.go's briefStubBody verbatim, and the skill's description of the machine-managed '## Your job' verdict-JSON contract (verdict required; hard_block optional boolean, top-level sibling of verdict; reviewer_id/confidence/rationale/concrete_changes_required/findings presentation-only) matches renderBriefHeader's actual output exactly — no drift, no skill-re-authored second verdict block found anywhere in either SKILL.md. (3) panel tally exit-code semantics empirically confirmed: Allow->0 (6/6 APPROVE, exit 0), Warn->0 with advisory printed to stderr (missing target branch, exit 0), Block->non-zero (stale reviewed_head_sha, exit 1) with the exact recovery line cmd/mindspec/panel.go's tallyExitAction emits — all matching the skill's 'Allow -> 0; Warn -> 0 ...; Block -> non-zero ...' description word for word.

findings.description: Empirically verified: `mindspec panel create` CLI grammar/flags match the ms-panel-run skill's prescribed invocation exactly (built binary, ran create + re-panel against a /tmp scratch repo).

Empirically verified: the BRIEF.md stub `create` writes (headings + machine-managed '## Your job' verdict-JSON contract, including hard_block as a top-level boolean sibling of verdict) matches the skill's description of it exactly, with no second skill-authored verdict-JSON block.
Empirically verified all three `panel tally` exit-code paths (Allow=0, Warn=0+advisory, Block=1+recovery line) match the ms-panel-tally skill's stated semantics.
